headline: State governments bypass 11 environmental requirements through gazette orders
context: At least six state governments used emergency gazette orders to bypass 11 federal environmental requirements for development projects between 2023 and 2025.

cards.big: 6 states used emergency gazette to skip 11 environmental rules for development
cards.sub: State land powers weaponised to circumvent federal environmental protections

cards.big: Land and forests fall under state list but EIA requirements are federal law
cards.sub: Constitutional tension allows states to approve what federal law should prevent

cards.big: Projects approved without EIA include RM3.4 billion in hillside developments
cards.sub: Environmental shortcuts create long-term disaster risk that costs far more than projects generate

cards.big: DOE issued objection letters for 8 of 11 cases but had no enforcement mechanism
cards.sub: Federal environmental agency can object but cannot stop state-approved projects

cards.big: When states can override environmental law, the environment has no government
Should federal environmental standards override state land approval powers?
